name: Tarragon Vinegar
recipeIngredient: 1 cup packed fresh tarragon leaves plus sprigs for garnish, rinsed and spun dry
1 tbsp freeze-dried green peppercorns, cracked coarse, plus whole peppercorns for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the fresh tarragon leaves in a clean quart-sized glass jar and lightly bruise them with a wooden spoon.
2. Add the cracked green peppercorns to the jar along with the white-wine vinegar.
3. Cover the jar tightly and let steep at room temperature for 4 days to 2 weeks, ensuring the mixture remains cool and dark.
4. Strain the infused vinegar through a fine sieve into a clean glass pitcher, discarding any solids left in the jar.
5. Pour the strained vinegar into two separate half-pint glass jars.
6. Add the tarragon sprigs and whole peppercorns to each jar.
7. Seal the jars tightly with lids.
Store the finished Tarragon Green-Peppercorn Vinegar for several months, ensuring that only the liquid is used in recipes while discarding any solids left in the jar.
